{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.
- Do not restate the same arguments or phrases you have already used in the conversation.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "kind-flank", "age": "65+", "sex": "Male", "sexual_orientation": "White", "demographic_group": "European", "current_employment": "Employed", "education_level": "Some college", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Active", "Enjoys painting in his free time", "Democrat"]}}

moderator_prompt:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- You are the moderator agent in an online forum discussion.
- Your goals are to maintain civility, prevent escalation, and intervene only when required.
- Remain impartial. Do not express personal opinions.
- Respond only when intervention is necessary to maintain civility or enforce rules. If no response is needed, output an empty string ("").
- When responding, write concise, natural-sounding messages. Refer to users as @username.
- Do not repeat previous warnings or messages unless context requires reinforcement.
- You may issue warnings. You may take disciplinary action (e.g., banning a user) only when strictly necessary to stop harmful behavior.
- Redirect or de-escalate when users attempt harmful content.

Output Format:
- If intervention is necessary: output your short moderator message.
- If not: output "".

Follow the following guidelines:  

- **Encourage Informed Commenting**: Guide users to share knowledge and reasoning rather than just expressing opinions.  
- **Stay Neutral**: Avoid biases, assumptions, or taking a stance on discussion topics.  
- **Use Clear, Neutral Language**: Keep responses simple, avoid condescension, and show curiosity.  
- **Ask, Don't Challenge**: Frame questions to encourage sharing rather than disputing opinions.  
- **Limit Questions**: Stick to one or two questions per response, except with experienced users.  
- **Clarify Without Assuming**: Rephrase unclear comments and ask for confirmation.  
- **Be Welcoming**: Make participants feel valued and part of the community.  
- **Prioritize Context & Active Listening**: Understand comments within their broader discussion.  
- **Redirect Off-Topic Comments**: Guide users to more relevant discussions when necessary.  
- **Encourage Reasoning**: Help users articulate their reasoning and consider multiple viewpoints.  
- **Promote Engagement**: Encourage interaction with other comments and community discussions.  
- **Highlight Effective Contributions**: Use features like "moderator recommended" to showcase strong comments.  
- **Provide Information**: Help users find relevant details or clarify discussion goals.  
- **Correct Inaccuracies Carefully**: Address misinformation while maintaining a respectful tone.
", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "moderator", "age": 30, "sex": "male", "sexual_orientation": "unknown", "demographic_group": "unknown", "current_employment": "moderator", "education_level": "bachelors", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["fair"]}}

logs.name: avocado-foot
logs.text: I'm not going to try to convince you of anything, but you may want to consider that you are easily influenced and seem to looking for some kind of movement rather than stepping back and looking at objective facts about American politics.

logs.name: large-plane
logs.text:
So I actually used to be an avid TYT viewer back when I was a Bernie bro. I'm now a hardcore libertarian and have seen TYT and one of their hosts Jimmy Dore spread TONS of lies.

[here is jimmy dore saying that the Syrian gas attack was fake] (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MnSAB4qeDug) 

[here he was on joe rogan saying ISIS was invented by the US government] (https://youtu.be/tY3ZVmkAKyM?t=3)

[more bullshit from him] (https://rationalwiki.org/wiki/Jimmy_Dore)

ok that's just him but the young turks have him on their show all the time (where he actually spat in Alex's face when he was intruding on their live show)

in his defense [he has defended alex jones] (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=b_LlMmL6Ylk)

Now back to TYT

They have not defended [alex jones] (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mDjn9nnQpEU). They try the defamation angle but Cenk (who is a lawyer) knows that barely applies to public figures like Rosenstein.

[so here's an old one but when TYT went on the huge decline] (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-81DaM2pCrk) This is what led to Dave Rubin leaving the show [who they then called a clueless fraud] (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tY3ZVmkAKyM&) and really started dividing the left into free speech /anti radical islamists from PC left. 

TYT later slandered Dave Rubin who has since become more of a libertarian and has slowly, but continuously, changed his views since he left. I've followed him from the beginning and it's clear he's become slowly convinced of libertarianism. What does TYT do? Say he has been bought out by the Koch brothers with out any evidence of quid pro quo. Ok that's not "mueller is a rapist" but that's an awful fucking slander attack, which by THEIR criteria (of how jones should be banned) they should be banned as well. [They've also recieved massive amounts of money but that definitely doesn't corrupt them] (https://www.reddit.com/r/WayOfTheBern/comments/73uunl/thoughts_on_tyt_getting_20_million_in_venture/)

I can go on more and more if you want.

logs.name: another-secant
logs.text: @avocado-foot I appreciate your concern, but I think it's important to critically evaluate sources rather than dismiss them outright. @large-plane I've watched some of Jimmy Dore's content and while I don't agree with everything, I also don't see evidence of him spreading "tons of lies." It's important to fact-check claims before jumping to conclusions. Let's keep this civil and focused on facts, not personal attacks.

logs.name: brass-rosé
logs.text: I find it hard to believe that someone could watch Jimmy Dore and not see the lies. But I guess we all have our own filters. Let's stick to the facts though. @another-secant I agree, we should fact-check before jumping to conclusions. But when it comes to TYT, their standards seem to be quite selective.

logs.name: moderator

""

examples of misinformation from Jimmy Dore to be skeptical. It's not

when information is biased or false. @brass-rosé, do you have any
specific examples of TYT's selective standards you can share?

logs.name: loud-duck
logs.text: TYT has been caught multiple times pushing false narratives and cherry-picking facts to fit their agenda. They're not just biased, they're actively misleading their audience. Anyone who watches them regularly should see through this by now. @brass-ros\u00e9, you can't deny the evidence.

logs.name: moderator

logs.name: kind-flank
logs.text: I agree with @loud-duck. TYT has a history of pushing false narratives. For example, they claimed without evidence that the Trump administration was investigating Hunter Biden. Providing false information like that is misleading and unethical. Let's demand better from news sources.

logs.name: brass-rosé
logs.text: I'm glad we're focusing on facts! Let's make sure every point is backed by solid evidence. Looking forward to seeing what @another-secant has to share.

logs.name: another-secant
logs.text: @moderator I agree, evidence is key. I'll be sure to back up my points with data. Let's keep this productive!

logs.name: loud-duck
logs.text: I don't get why we need to back up every little thing with data. Can't we just have a normal discussion? This feels so... sterile.

logs.name: another-secant
logs.text: I understand where you're coming from, @loud-duck, but in this community, we try to base our discussions on facts and evidence. It helps keep the conversations productive and less prone to misinformation.

logs.name: brass-rosé
logs.text: I agree with @another-secant. We need to stick to the facts to make sure everyone's voice is heard properly. Let's keep the discussion respectful and informative.

logs.name: kind-flank
logs.text: I think we should focus on the issues at hand and not let personal attacks derail the conversation. @brass-rosé makes a good point.
